title: Top 1000 Japanese verbs for recognition reading practice
selectionRules: Start from a rank-ordered BCCWJ-derived frequency list.
Keep only exact surface+reading pairs that match modern verb entries in EDICT/JMdict-style dictionary data.
Exclude suru-family and noun-plus-suru verbs from the V1 core deck.
Require kanji+hiragana orthography for the tracked V1 item.
Keep only words with origin 和 / 漢 / 混 in the working frequency list.
Quality-gate candidates by keeping entries that are either common in the dictionary source or present in NINJAL educational/basic-vocabulary lists.
Collapse to one selected entry per orthographic form for V1 mastery tracking. Keep alternate readings as metadata.
Select the first 1000 unique orthographic verbs after the above filters.
notes: This is a transformed seed dataset for a static educational app.
Inflected forms are mechanically generated from the selected lemma spelling, with special-case overrides for 来る, 有る, and honorific -aru verbs.
Some verbs have multiple readings for the same spelling. Those alternate readings are kept in metadata for later UI review.
